top_signals.title: Brendan Greeley on the Real 500-Year History of the Dollar | Odd Lots
top_signals.summary: We love talking about money. And of course we love talking about the dollar, in all its varieties, from bank deposits to eurodollars to stablecoins. But what fundamentally is a dollar and who actually controls it? Before you can even talk about any of that, you need to understand how the dollar was born. Brendan Greeley argues the dollar is a lot older than you might suspect: It is a 500-year-old idea. The word itself has an incredible etymology: It's derived from German, but refers to a Spanish currency made from silver found in Mexico. The story of the dollar, Greeley tells us, goes way beyond the US and implicates the whole world. Our conversation coincides with the release of Greely's new book, 'The Almighty Dollar: 500 Years of the World's Most Powerful Money.' (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: China AI Upstart MiniMax Doubles Sales Ahead of New Model Launch
top_signals.summary: MiniMax Group Inc.’s annualized revenue more than doubled over the past two months, as the Chinese AI upstart plans to roll out its next flagship model to entice developers and enterprise clients.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Accredited official statistics: HM Treasury: 2026 COVID-19 Cost Tracker update
top_signals.summary: Official Statistics on the cost of measures announced in response to the COVID-19 pandemic and how much the government has spent on these measures so far.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
